NOTE: a different Walter Weathers is a Managing Director at Pelican Energy Partners (Houston oil-and-gas private equity); that person is actively employed as an energy executive and therefore does NOT match the 'Not Employed' occupation on this filing, so no oil_gas affiliation is recorded here.

name: Siff, Ted
resolved_employer: Creating Common Ground (principal; private-lands conservation consultancy, 604 W. 11th St., Austin 78701); formerly owner/president Park Place Publications, LP
evidence: Ted Siff is a long-documented Austin parks/open-space advocate whose consultancy Creating Common Ground is registered at 604 W. 11th Street, Austin 78701 - matching the filing zip and stated occupation. Career corroborated across multiple independent bios: Texas Director of the Trust for Public Land (hired 1991 to run its Austin office), board member then executive director of the Austin Parks Foundation (2001-2005), former senior manager/part owner of Austin publishing companies including Third Coast Magazine, The Quorum Report and Texas Monthly, and executive director of Victor Emanuel Nature Tours. His public footprint is entirely conservation/parks civic advocacy; no federal PAC itemizations, no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration, and no Israel/Palestine, firearms, oil-and-gas or defense affiliations were found.

name: Russell, James
resolved_employer: Trail of Lights Foundation (executive director); previously executive director, RunTex Foundation
evidence: James Russell is the executive director of the Austin Trail of Lights Foundation, the independent nonprofit that has produced the Trail of Lights in partnership with the City of Austin since 2013 after he worked with the city to revive the event following its 2009 cancellation; he previously served as executive director of the RunTex Foundation, which took the event over in 2012. He also holds a City of Austin policy role: he was seated on the Parkland Events Task Force as an Open Space, Environment & Sustainability Committee nominee and served as co-chair of the Special Events Task Force shaping Austin's special-events ordinance. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration and no itemized federal PAC contributions were found; no Israel/Palestine, firearms, oil-and-gas or defense affiliations found.

name: Guerra, Terrysa
resolved_employer: United for Respect Education Fund — Co-Executive Director
evidence: Terrysa Guerra is Co-Executive Director of United for Respect (and its Education Fund), matching the donor's employer string exactly; the first name is distinctive and her biography is Texas-rooted (raised in Los Fresnos, TX; Texas A&M graduate). Before joining United for Respect in 2018 she worked for the national and Texas state Democratic parties, managed campaigns for two Texas state senators and a state representative, and was deputy campaign manager for Wendy Davis's gubernatorial bid. At United for Respect she built the organization's policy and political programs — retail-worker organizing, guaranteed severance pay, scheduling standards, and the Toys R Us worker hardship fund. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found under her name. FEC search run: the pipeline records 20 federal donations; no ideological PAC ties on any side of the Israel/Palestine, firearms, energy, or defense spectrums surfaced, and committee-level detail was not retrievable (FEC browser is a JS app; open FEC API returned HTTP 429).
